title: Excessive Data Exposure via Unprotected API Endpoints
description: Multiple API endpoints expose excessive sensitive data without adequate authentication or authorization. The /api/Users endpoint returns all user records including email addresses, bcrypt password hashes, security questions/answers, and other PII. Other endpoints like /api/Cards (credit card info), /api/Feedbacks, /api/Challenges, /api/Products, and /rest/admin/application-configuration return more data than necessary, often without requiring authentication.
poc: Step 1: GET /api/Users without any auth headers returns HTTP 200 with JSON array of all users including bcrypt password hashes. Step 2: GET /api/Cards returns saved credit card data. Observed: sensitive data exposed without authentication.
proof: Observed excessive data exposure on multiple API endpoints. GET /api/Users returns all user records including email, password (bcrypt hash), role, securityQuestion, securityAnswer without authentication. GET /api/Cards returns saved credit card information. GET /api/Challenges returns all challenge definitions.

proof_excerpts.excerpt: Observed excessive data exposure on multiple API endpoints. GET /api/Users returns all user records including email, password (bcrypt hash), role, securityQuestion, securityAnswer without authentication. GET /api/Cards returns saved credit card information. GET /api/Challenges returns all challenge definitions.
